steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find the missing width
steps.content: The base area is $56t$ and the length is $\frac{14}{3}$, so $$\text{width}=56t\div\frac{14}{3}=56t\cdot\frac{3}{14}=12t.$$

steps.title: Find the three face areas
steps.content: The base area is $56t$. The front face area is $$\frac{14}{3}\cdot3=14.$$ The side face area is $$12t\cdot3=36t.$$

steps.title: Double the face areas
steps.content: A rectangular prism has two of each face, so the surface area is $$2(56t+14+36t)=2(92t+14)=184t+28.$$
